heading: Acquisition of property
label: 230a Acquisition of property
notes: Amendments2009—Subsec. (a). , inserted heading, inserted par. (1) designation and heading and substituted text of par. (1) for “Within the Barataria Marsh Unit the Secretary is authorized to acquire not to exceed eight thousand six hundred acres of lands, waters, and interests therein (hereinafter referred to as the ‘core area’), as depicted on the map referred to in , by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, or exchange.”, inserted par. (2) designation and heading and substituted “The Secretary may acquire by any of the methods referred to in paragraph (1)(A)” for “The Secretary may also acquire by any of the foregoing methods”, inserted par. children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may acquire any land, water, and interests in land and water within the Barataria Preserve Unit by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, transfer from any other Federal agency, or exchange.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any non-Federal land depicted on the map described in as “Lands Proposed for Addition” may be acquired by the Secretary only with the consent of the owner of the land.section 230 of this title

children.heading: Boundary adjustment
children.label: ii Boundary adjustment
children.text: On the date on which the Secretary acquires a parcel of land described in clause (i), the boundary of the Barataria Preserve Unit shall be adjusted to reflect the acquisition.

children.heading: Easements
children.label: iii Easements
children.text: To ensure adequate hurricane protection of the communities located in the area, any land identified on the map described in that is acquired or transferred shall be subject to any easements that have been agreed to by the Secretary and the Secretary of the Army.section 230 of this title

children.heading: Transfer of administration jurisdiction
children.label: C Transfer of administration jurisdiction
children.text: Effective on , administrative jurisdiction over any Federal land within the areas depicted on the map described in as “Lands Proposed for Addition” is transferred, without consideration, to the administrative jurisdiction of the National Park Service, to be administered as part of the Barataria Preserve Unit.March 30, 2009section 230 of this title

children.heading: French Quarter
children.label: 2 French Quarter
children.text: The Secretary may acquire by any of the methods referred to in paragraph (1)(A) such lands and interests therein, including leasehold interests, as he may designate in the French Quarter of New Orleans for development and operation as an interpretive and administrative facility.

children.heading: Acquisition of State land
children.label: 3 Acquisition of State land
children.text: Land, water, and interests in land and water owned by the State of Louisiana or any political subdivision thereof may be acquired only by donation.

children.heading: Acquisition of oil and gas rights
children.label: 4 Acquisition of oil and gas rights
children.text: In acquiring property pursuant to this part, the Secretary may not acquire right to oil and gas without the consent of the owner, but the exercise of such rights shall be subject to such regulations as the Secretary may promulgate in furtherance of the purposes of this part.
the integrity of ecological and biological systems; and
acquire land, water, and interests in land and water, by any of the methods referred to in subsection (a)(1)(A) (including use of appropriations from the Land and Water Conservation Fund); and
revise the boundaries of the Barataria Preserve Unit to include adjacent land and water.

children.heading: Acadian villages and towns
children.label: d Acadian villages and towns
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to acquire lands or interests in lands by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds or exchange, not to exceed approximately 20 acres, in Acadian villages and towns. Any lands so acquired shall be developed, maintained and operated as part of the Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve.
